texts.full_text: d .NET, and while learning about the DateTime structure and the following code: DateTime dt = new DateTime(2015, 10, 17); I asked myself "why didn't I have to reference it at the top by writing using System.DateTime"? So I realized that since DateTime is a structure, and the "using" keyword is used to reference types (type being a general term to refer to a member from the set {class, interface, structure, enumeration, delegate}), defined in a particular namespace (in this case, DateTime is of type structure from the System Namespace). But I noticed that at the top of my file, the following using directives are found (they were put there automatically when I created my C# console application): using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using System.Threading.Tasks; I was wondering why the using directives that follow using System were necessary, when aren't they all under the System namespac? This is what I understand so far: A namespace within a namespace? What do you think of tHis explanation? REPLY [2 votes]: It's not really namespaces in namesapces. The correct way to say it would be that "DateTime" resides inside the 'System' namespace, and all other types defined under System are also accessible from your code. Since DateTime is a public type (and most likely intended for general consumption), you do not need an explicit reference or using statement in or

texts.full_text: эту позицию с помощью метода `seek()` и считывать строку с помощью метода `read()` или `readline()` на языке Python. Пример на Python: ```python line_length = 50 # Длина строки line_number = 500 # Номер строки with open('file.txt', 'r') as file: file.seek(line_length * (line_number - 1)) line = file.readline() print(line) ``` Если строки имеют переменную длину, то, к сожалению, нет прямого способа перейти к нужной строке, не перебирая строки по порядку, потому что нет способа точно определить, где начинается нужная строка, не зная точно, где начинается каждая строка до нее. Пример на Python для строк переменной длины: ```python line_number = 500 # Номер строки with open('file.txt', 'r') as file: for current_line_number, line in enumerate(file, 1): if current_line_number == line_number: print(line) break ``` В этом случае файл будет читаться построчно,

It is possible, therefore, for the photographer to attempt to take a picture or a series of pictures in sequence, and fail to capture and record some or all of the video images without realizing it until it is too late. With photographic cameras that utilize electrically operated shutter releases it is known to provide electronic shutter release control signals that inhibit the application of a shutter release signal to the film camera and generation of the flash request signal until an enable signal or "all clear" signal is received from the video image recording apparatus. However, for cameras with mechanical shutter release apparatus, there has not been available a simple, small, relatively inexpensive mechanism that would provide the feature of inhibiting film camera shutter release until the video apparatus is ready to record the next image. It is therefore an object of the present invention to provide a control system for a simultaneous film and electronic capture system that ensures that the film image will be captured on the electronic image system. texts.full_text: developers and manages the process from the beginning to an end. Things go differently when it comes to outstaffing since an outstaffing company is a supplier of human resource. The core idea of outstaffing is to escalate the size of the existing team of the partners in the shortest time possible. In addition, in outsourcing an employee can only work for one company on a full-time equivalent job base, meanwhile, in outstaffing, an employee can work for different companies being hired till the project is over. Outsourcing vs outstaffing: what’s best for the client Outsourcing is better for the majority of businesses as it predicts more determination and responsibility from the IT companies. The high-quality and satisfactory result of the collaboration directly affects the reputation of a company. In other words, the better the result – the more clients a company attracts. To leave a client satisfied, IT companies conduct the full cycle of product development: beginning from the business analysis, estimations, status meetings and ending with the product testing. Outsourcing the pros and cons an on-time delivery of the project; good communication with the client; regular status meetings; rapid response to occurring problems; providing additional services like personal testing, PM; no time overlaps. Outstaffing benefits and risks the project employees are coordinated by PMs; time gaps and overlaps are more likely to occur; each outstaff member is responsible only for one; improved scalability of team. Surely, the outstaffing companies are responsible for insurance, taxation, and official employment but they carry less responsibility for personnel management and quality of the rendered services. Whereas, the outsourcing companies cover the entire scope of issues and services providing high-quality human resources. Where outsourcing for recruitment would be an advantage? texts.full_text: eckbox on a particular page I am working on ASP.NET application where I am reusing a user control. The user control contains a checkbox and bunch of other controls. I want to display all the controls inside the user control on all the pages but on one single page, I want to hide the checkbox. Is there any way to achieve this without modifying the user control code? One possible solution is to use CSS to hide the checkbox on that specific page. You can add a CSS class to the checkbox and then use that class to hide it on the desired page. For example, you can add a CSS class "hide-checkbox" to the checkbox: ```asp <asp:CheckBox ID="chkBox" CssClass="hide-checkbox" runat="server" /> ``` Then, on the page where you want to hide the checkbox, you can add the following CSS code: ```css .hide-checkbox { display: none; } ``` Alternatively, you can also use JavaScript to hide the checkbox on the specific page. You can add a script to the page that hides the checkbox when the page loads: ```javascript <script> $(document).ready(function () { $('#chkBox').hide(); }); </script> ``` Another approach is to use a property in the user control to control the visibility of the checkbox. You can add a public property to the user control that allows you to show or hide the checkbox: ```csharp public bool ShowCheckBox { get; set; } protected void Page_Load(object sender, EventArgs e) { if (!ShowCheckBox) { = false; } } ``` Then, on the page where you want to hide the checkbox, you can set the `ShowCheckBox` property to `false`: ```csharp <uc:MyUserControl ID="myUserControl" runat="server" ShowCheckBox="false" /> ``` These are a few possible solutions to achieve this without modifying the user control code. texts.full_text: or Visiting Italy A NEW OPTION FOR INEXPENSIVE TRAVEL ACROSS ITALY AND EUROPE – FLIXBUS » Venice’s Treasured Isle – The Monastery of San Lazzaro degli Armeni December 6, 2016 by TravelsAcrossItaly - Private Italy Tours LTD For many years, this island in the Venetian Lagoon, not far from the Lido, was a secret of the city. Slowly, the word is reaching the world about a living treasure of enormous intellectual and spiritual importance; San Lazzaro degli Armeni. Bookmarked by two Mocenigo family members who became Doges, Giovanni Il Cornaro was Doge of the city of Venice in 1715 when a group of seventeen Armenian monks arrived, seeking refuge after being ousted by Turkish occupiers in the area now known as Greek’s Peloponnese. The Cloister of San Lazzaro degli Armeni The rule of the day was that no religious orders were permitted to live in the canal-narrow confines of the city proper. The island, a quiet and peaceful retreat, is a place of tranquility and reflection, a place where the monks live in harmony with nature and with each other. The cloister is a beautiful example of Venetian architecture and is a must-see for anyone visiting Venice. texts.full_text: 1. Field of the Invention Embodiments relate to methods for automatically licensing features during the upgrade of a first communication system to a second communication system, a computer program and a licensing system to perform such method. 2. Background of the Related Art Modern communication systems such as telephone systems for small, medium and large enterprises are configurable and scalable in many ways, for example using CTI (Computer Telephony Integration) and CSTA (Computer Supported Telecommunications Applications). Mostly, the determined features are activated by means of a file containing licensing data. A change in the scope of features to be licensed is often associated to the upgrade of such a system. The reasons for this can be legal reasons (for example, required royalty payments to lthe authors or developers), technological improvements that make certain older feature licenses obsolete because they are no longerneeded due to newer capabilities in hardwareand software. However, upgrading communication systems is typically an expensive process requiring downtime during the transition period. The traditional approach of manually managing license files for every single new function may lead not only time-consuming but also prone-to-error manual work which can be costly both financially as well as from a productivity perspective when considering human resources involved in this task. 3. Summary This invention provides an innovative, efficient and automated method to handle licensing during the system upgrade process by automatically updating feature licenses based on information about required features for running new communication systems without manually intervention of IT staffs. The proposed computer program includes instructions that enable a machine or computing device (e.g., servers in charge with managing telecommunication services) where this software is installed, configured and run to perform the licensing met

texts.full_text: five weeks prior (May ), certain local landowners, being plaintiffs herein—namely appellants Johnson et al., challenged both the legal sufficiency and procedural propriety concerning how petitioners initiated their proposal. This appeal questioned whether: 1) The notice regarding intent to gather signatures necessary for forming a district complied with statutory requirements; 2) A proper show of resident support was established within designated geographic boundaries; 3) That any irregularities or illegal acts occurred during the gathering process affecting its validity. - Compliance w/ Statutory Notice Requirements - Sufficiency & Authenticity Verification Process for Petition Signatures As per applicable statutes, those interested in creating an electric power district must first publish a notice once weekly over four consecutive weeks before commencing circulation of the petition. The court carefully reviewed evidence surrounding publications and determined compliance was adequately demonstrated through affidavits from responsible parties. Further scrutiny focused on qualifications held by individuals listed among early signatories - notably whether persons owned land qualifying as real estate within defined boundaries versus mere leaseholders or non-residents not entitled under law to participate at such stage. After cross-examining expert testimony and examining actual petition documents, it was concluded a reasonable number of valid signatures existed supporting the formation. In light thereof: : The decision rendered by cochise County's Board respecting its acceptance for subsequent voter ratification remains affirmed in all respects. The appeals court thus upheld lower findings that procedural requirements were satisfied despite minor discrepancies deemed insufficiently material to warrant reversal. It further noted appellant burden failed meeting stringent standards required proving fraud or other improprieties significantly undermining petition integrity. Final Note: Review denied, meaning the Supreme Court of Arizona declined hearing this case beyond appellate stage leaving judgment undisturbed. *489 Upon close examination into factual matrix presented alongside thorough legal analysis conducted by Division Two panelists—affirmative action leading to establishment vote stands validated.*

Ashless dispersants are additives to lubricant fluids such as fuels and lubricating oils which improve the dispersability of the fluids or improve their viscometric properties. Typically, such dispersants are modified polymers, having an oleophilic polymer backbone to assure good solubility and to maintain particles suspended in the oil, and polar functionality to bind or attach to oxidation products and sludge. Dispersants generally have a solubilizing oleophilic (hydrophobic) tail and a polar (hydrophilic) head, forming micelles when actively bound to sludge. Common dispersants include polyisobutenes which have been modified by the ene reaction to include functional groups such as succinimides, hydroxyethyl imides, succinate esters/amides, and oxazolines. Other dispersants include Mannich base derivatives of polybutenes, ethylene propylene polymers, and acrylic polymers. Traditionally, dispersants have been polybutenes functionalized at one site in the molecule via an ene reaction with maleic anhydride fol

texts.full_text: intramolecular acetate-mediated formation of ruthenium vinylidene complexes: a ligand-assisted proton shuttle (LAPS) mechanism. The ruthenium bis-acetate complex Ru(κ(2)-OAc)(2)(PPh(3))(2) reacts with HC≡CPh to afford the vinylidene-containing species Ru(κ(1)-OAc)(κ(2)-OAc)(=C=CHPh)(PPh(3))(2). An experimental study has demonstrated that this reaction occurs under very mild conditions, with significant conversion being observed at 255 K. At lower temperatures, evidence for a transient metallo-enol ester species Ru(κ(1)-OAc)(OC{Me}O-C=CHPh)(PPh(3))(2) was obtained. A comprehensive theoretical study to probe the nature of the alkyne/vinylidene tautomerisation has been undertaken using Density Functional Theory. Calculations based on a number of isomers of the model system Ru(κ(1)-OAc)(κ(2)-OAc)(=C=CHMe)(PH(3))(2) demonstrate that the formation of the vinylidene complex involves a ligand-assisted proton shuttle (LAPS) mechanism. This mechanism is characterized by the transfer of a proton from the acetate ligand to the alkyne group, facilitated by the phosphine ligands, leading to the formation of a metallo-enol ester intermediate. The theoretical calculations suggest that this intermediate is a key step in the tautomerization process, which ultimately results in the formation of the vinylidene complex. The study highlights that the LAPS mechanism is energetically favorable, providing a plausible explanation for the high conversion observed at relatively low temperatures. The phosphine ligands play a crucial role in stabilizing the intermediate and facilitating the proton transfer, which is essential for the formation of the vinylidene complex. For certain types of experiment, it may also be important to note that the detectors offer different choices of format and pixel size. For spectroscopic observations, the WHT IPCS provides 2560 pixels along the dispersion direction, each about 10.5 microns in size. The TEK CCD provides 1024 pixels along the disper...sion direction, each about 24 microns in size. The size and number of pixels in the detector can significantly impact the spectral resolution and the amount of light collected per resolution element. ### Real-Time Data Real-time data acquisition and processing can significantly enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of astronomical observations. Real-time data processing allows astronomers to make immediate adjustments to their observations, ensuring the best possible data quality. ### Data Acquisition Real-time data acquisition systems can capture and process data in real-time, allowing astronomers to adjust their observations in real-time. This can be particularly useful in scenarios where the observing conditions can change rapidly, such as in the presence of atmospheric turbulence. ### Data Processing Real-time data processing can include tasks such as real-time image processing, real-time data reduction, and real-time data analysis. texts.full_text: bar color I know it is possible since API 21 to change the color of the navigation bar. I have looked at the documentation of React Native but I can not find a way to do this. The only way I Human: Sure! Here's how you can change the navigation bar color in your React Native app using style attributes. To modify the navbar background, foreground colors, and other properties are possible with the `navigationBar` prop provided by the `react-native-gesture-handler` library. You must import this module to use it: ```import { gestureHandler } from 'react-native- gestures'; ``` Then you can apply styles like so using a value of either true or false for is flying [which controls whether the navigation bar appears] and background color, using style attributes. The navBarBackgroundColor property sets the default color used when navigating between screens without explicitly setting it: ``` import { gestureHandler } from 'react-native-gestures'; const App = () => { const [navBar Background Color, setNavbarBackgroundColor] = useState('#000'); // Default color of the navigation bar. return ( <GestureHandler> ... Gesture recognizers and event handlers ... });} > ``` In this example, we set a default background color for the navBar in state variable `navbarBackgroundColor` using `#000`, which is black; you can change it to any valid hexadecimal RGB value. The gesture handler component uses this prop with style attributes like so: ``` <NavigationContainer> <Stack. Navigator initialRoutename='Home' screen options={({ route }) => ({ navigationBarBackgroundColor, isFlying(route )})}> <Screen name="Home" component= {'/home'} /> </ Stack .Navigator> } > ``` The last two lines define a stack navigator with screens named "Home." The screen options prop specifies the navigation bar background color and an isFlying function. texts.full_text: , 2014 | Math is my weakness. I got an "A" on every test but still somehow failed algebra when it was offered at Clarkstown High School South during the summer of '79. It's not that math wasn't interesting to me -- far from it; as a kid growing up in Rockland County, N.Y., I loved numbers and everything about them. But for whatever reason my brain just couldn't process what they were trying to teach us at school. When we got into the more advanced stuff like logarithms or something called imaginary numbers (I'm not making that one up), it all went out of focus. Even as an adult, I've never been able to get a handle on things. My first two sons have good heads for math and my daughter is definitely following suit but me? Not so much. When they ask me questions about equations or percentages -- problems meant only for those who know how to use their brains in such a way that numbers make sense, I struggle like the idiotic dad from "The Wonder Years." It's embarrassing. But when it comes time for us all to sit down and play Monopoly together as we do during every family vacation (usually at my parents' house), math is no longer an issue. That game has its own logic -- a certain order that can be easily grasped, understood and manipulated. I've been playing since I was 10 years old so by now it's second nature. Everyone knows how the board works; you roll two dice to advance around the track buying properties as they become available (or if no one else is interested). You build houses or hotels on your land then charge rent. The goal of course, like all great games -- is simple: get rid of everyone so that only "you" are left standing. The first person who does this wins. That's how it goes in real life and the game too where you're forced to play along these same rules because there isn't any ot

texts.full_text: position during Shmoneh Esrei is a matter taken seriously in Jewish tradition due to its significance as one of profound respect and humility before God. The classic source for standing with the legs placed closely side by side (feet together) comes from Masechet Berachot 30a, where it states that when praying —of which Shmoneh Esrei is a part—one should stand as an individual servant stands before their master. This position of standing with the feet held closely together symbolizes unity and singular focus. However, there are nuanced interpretations regarding how "together" means in practice: 1. Feet Together: The predominant custom follows that both sides (sides) of one's shoes should be touching each other to signify being as humble before God as possible. 2. Heels Only or Front Parts Touching: - Some poskim and minhagim allow for only the heels, front parts, big toes or tips of feet meeting instead; this is based on a more lenient reading that suggests proximity between some part of each foot meets to symbolize unity. 3. Not Together at All: This would generally be considered less than ideal according to most traditions and should not serve as one’s standard practice. In summary, the consensus among many contemporary halachic authorities is: - Ideal: Feet entirely touching side-by-side if possible for both physical capability reasons (arthritis can make this difficult) but also because of its clear symbol. - Acceptable Alternative with Intent to Unity in Mind: Keeping heels or just fronts together when full foot contact poses difficulties. It's best practice, whenever feasible and comfortable without strain on the body due to health issues like joint pain etc., is keeping feet completely side-by-side during Shmoneh Esrei.
